codeReview.summary: The commit adds strong end-to-end traceability integration tests and transcript evidence for matrix/navigation checks, but it does not verify the required empty-state behavior in the UI itself.

issues.description: Expected behavior includes "Empty state renders gracefully when no links exist," but the added coverage only validates an empty API payload (GET /api/traceability) and does not exercise `/traceability/` empty-state rendering. Transcript evidence also shows this UI check was deferred to API test coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/architecture.md is stale and says traceability pages are not implemented, which is no longer true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:69 says "ER diagram and traceability pages are not yet implemented", while `web/src/pages/traceability.astro` exists in the repo.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The integration-worker guidance could more explicitly require UI acceptance criteria to be verified in the UI variant itself (not substituted with API-only assertions).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl (session 144c704b-382a-48f2-9b32-d4685ca661c9, line 19) includes: "Let me check the empty state through the test (the cross-flow test already covers this)."

summary: Reviewed m2-cross-flows via handoff, commit 6a278dc diff, and transcript skeleton. The implementation thoroughly validates backend cross-flow behavior and reports manual UI navigation checks, but it does not directly verify the required empty-state UI rendering behavior, so the feature is marked fail.
